description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, featuring a rectangular wooden box as its main body. The front face displays two large metal bells and a circular mouthpiece, both made of dark metal, giving it a classic early 20th-century appearance. On one side, a black metal crank handle is visible, used for generating a signal, while the opposite side holds a separate earpiece attached by a straight cord. The wood is visibly aged, with a rough, weathered texture and some chipping along the edges, suggesting long-term use. The metal components, including the bells, mouthpiece, and earpiece, have a matte, slightly tarnished finish. This type of phone would typically be found mounted on a wall in older urban environments, such as historic homes or restored public buildings.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden box body, metal bells, and a separate earpiece on a cord. The surface is aged wood with dark, matte metal components.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden box, crank handle, and protruding metal mouthpiece.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the flat wooden back and the earpiece hanging to the side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the earpiece and its cord visible, attached to the wooden body.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the two large metal bells, circular mouthpiece, and the earpiece to the left.
